summary: Two-storey rectangular Minecraft building with a stepped brick gable-like roof, sandstone mid walls with a wooden belt between floors, corner stone pillars, and a raised white stone (likely quartz) border/foundation. Rows of narrow windows on both floors; building sits within a recessed dirt plot.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with a stepped centered roof ridge (symmetrical along length)
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along the long axis (front/back identical; left/right mostly identical)

elements: two full-length rows of narrow windows (ground and first floor) on the visible long sides
stepped brick roof with stone slab/trim at edges
raised white stone foundation/border around building footprint
recessed ground area/dirt moat around the foundation
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular sandstone box roughly 11x17 blocks footprint and 5 blocks tall for two interior floors (visible external height ≈ 6–7 before roof).
Place a continuous wooden plank strip around mid-height between the two floors to match the belt detail.
Add narrow 1x1 high glass windows spaced evenly in a horizontal band on each floor; leave small vertical stonebrick corner pillars 2–3 blocks tall between bands.
Construct a stepped brick roof: slope up from both long sides toward center with brick stairs/blocks, add stone slab trim at the eaves and ridge.
Surround the base with a 1-block-high white border (quartz or similar) raised above ground; set the surrounding ground down one block to create the recessed plot.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white trim assumed to be quartz based on color but could be wool/concrete; confidence 0.8.
Corner pillar material looks like stonebrick or andesite / polished variant; identification confidence 0.7.
Interior layout, door locations, and rear facades are not visible so internal floor heights and staircase placement are unknown.
Precise footprint dimensions estimated from pixel views; real block counts may differ by ±2 blocks.
